the_signal.headline: Fine-tuning vs. RAG: Production systems now need both
the_signal.body: Most GTM teams treating these as either/or when production LLM systems increasingly demand both approaches working together. Fine-tuning handles domain-specific behavior; RAG handles real-time data freshness. Your agent stack that only does one is leaving accuracy and cost optimization on the table.

tool_drop.name: Claude Code vs. n8n decision framework
tool_drop.what: n8n published a structured guide comparing Claude Code (fast prototyping) vs. n8n (production workflows with audit trails).
tool_drop.why: GTM teams building AI-powered sales workflows need this decision tree before committing to either path—wrong choice means rework at scale.

thread_of_day.title: Compliance automation now demands infrastructure you control
thread_of_day.takeaway: SaaS compliance tools create vendor lock-in risk; building flexible workflows on controlled infrastructure is becoming table stakes for regulated revenue systems.

quote.text: Context engineering for LLMs goes beyond prompt design—it's about managing context rot, budgeting tokens, and building reliable production AI agents.
quote.source: n8n Blog: Fix Agent Failures With Context Engineering

whats_moving.context: Production teams realizing single-approach LLM strategies fail under real-world data freshness and domain specificity demands.
Regulated GTM motions (audit trails, data governance) shifting from SaaS tools to self-hosted n8n workflows for control and flexibility.
AI security monitoring moving from detection-only to automated response workflows, treating agent failures like production incidents requiring SOC-grade triage.

act_on_this: Audit your current LLM implementation: are you using RAG, fine-tuning, or both? If just one, map out which production workflows are losing accuracy or freshness this week and prototype the hybrid approach.

numbers.stat: 75 capabilities expected in production agent development tools, but most platforms only deliver half
numbers.context: This capability gap explains why agent failures keep escalating—tools are missing core identity, execution reliability, and intent resolution features GTM teams need.
